nodes.label: AI as automation tool for independent task completion
nodes.grounding_span: Definitely delegate complete tasks to handle independently for now

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: the AI would produce an answer that looked correct at first glance and give a reference. The answer was always incorrect and the reference link was broken every time

nodes.label: AI efficiency vs. setup overhead
nodes.grounding_span: it would still take less time to do it myself than to use AI. Giving a lot of context and complex instructions takes quite a long time

nodes.label: AI as productivity enhancer freeing time for complex problems
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to human skills
nodes.grounding_span: I see it as a tool that can open up more time for humans to focus on more complex analytical and creative problems

nodes.label: AI proficiency as essential skill
nodes.grounding_span: every person should be proficient in interacting with AI in the same way we use personal computers

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: increase productivity and allow us to spend time on more complex issues

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability in high-stakes tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I want the correct answer every time

nodes.label: Professional growth and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I'm actively trying to develop tools that would allow AI to take over entire tasks/areas of work

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination and fabricated references
nodes.grounding_span: what was frustrating was that instead of admitting it couldn't find anything, the AI would produce an answer that looked correct at first glance and give a reference

nodes.label: Preference for manual execution over AI for high-accuracy tasks
nodes.grounding_span: When the task requires 100% accuracy and a very particular tone that needs context to understand, I rather just do it myself

nodes.label: Optimistic embrace of AI as productivity tool
nodes.grounding_span: I see it as a tool that can open up more time for humans to focus on more complex analytical and creative problems

nodes.label: Belief in universal AI proficiency
nodes.grounding_span: every person should be proficient in interacting with AI in the same way we use personal computers

nodes.label: Cost-benefit analysis of AI vs. manual effort
nodes.grounding_span: it would still take less time to do it myself than to use AI. Giving a lot of context and complex instructions takes quite a long time

nodes.label: Proactive tool-building mindset
nodes.grounding_span: I'm actively trying to develop tools that would allow AI to take over entire tasks/areas of work
